title: $1,000 in Meta (Facebook) at IPO (2012)
description: What $1,000 invested in Meta (then Facebook) on its IPO date of May 18, 2012 would be worth today. The IPO was widely considered a disaster — the stock fell from $38 to a low of $17.73 within 90 days. Early investors who held through the criticism recovered handsomely.
dividend_note: Meta did not pay a dividend until 2024.
max_drawdown_note: Meta fell ~76% between September 2021 and October 2022 during the metaverse pivot and digital advertising downturn.
educational_note: Facebook's IPO was a technical disaster (NASDAQ glitches delayed the open) and the stock crashed immediately. Buying and holding required ignoring: the IPO drop, Cambridge Analytica scandal (2018), a 76% crash in 2022, and the metaverse skepticism. All of those were buying opportunities in hindsight.
